mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through reduced aggregate demand and profit margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2011 labor market showing 134289k payroll level
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 4.0% represents a restrictive monetary stance relative to inflation (CPI at 197.8). When FEDFUNDS exceeds the inflation-adjusted neutral rate, real borrowing costs rise, dampening equity valuations and forward returns over 60 trading days through higher discount rates and reduced capital investment.
evidence_refs: fact:Real fed funds rate estimated positive in June 2011
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with low inflation
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean return > 0% with t-test p > 0.10) when trigger condition is met, or correlation between real FEDFUNDS and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 2014k units reflect construction demand and household formation expectations. A sustained level below 2.2 million annualized units signals weak housing demand that reduces construction employment, building material demand, and consumer wealth effects, producing negative equity returns over 5 trading days as forward-looking markets price in demand weakness.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 2.2 million annualized units for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following HOUST trigger are positive in more than 60% of instances, or Rank IC between HOUST level and 5-day forward returns is below 0.01 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 108.96 reflects manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side output. When INDPRO growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment remains elevated above 4.8%, it signals supply-side weakness without demand-pull inflation, creating stagflation risk that compresses equity valuations over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2011 INDPRO at 108.96 and UNRATE at 5.0%
regime_hint: stagflation risk environment with weak supply growth
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (INDPRO × UNRATE) shows Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 353866 million dollars represent consumer spending momentum. A month-over-month decline in RSAFS signals deteriorating consumer demand that reduces corporate revenue expectations and equity earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings revisions cascade through equity markets.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 62% of months where RSAFS declines, or Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 5.0% combined with flat payroll growth signals labor market slack. When UNRATE rises above 4.9% while PAYEMS growth remains below 0.2% month-over-month, it indicates accelerating joblessness that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative equity returns over 60 trading days through earnings downgrades.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2011 UNRATE at 5.0% and PAYEMS at 134289k
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean > 0% with t-test p > 0.10) when both trigger conditions are met, or the joint effect shows Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL at 197.8 combined with FEDFUNDS at 4.0% creates a real rate environment. When nominal FEDFUNDS falls below 3.5% while CPI year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5%, real rates turn negative, signaling monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2011 real rates estimated near zero
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean < 0.5% with t-test p > 0.10) when trigger is active, or Rank IC of real rate proxy with forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) at 11206.1 billion chained 2017 dollars reflects aggregate demand strength. When quarterly GDPC1 growth decelerates below 1.5% annualized while RSAFS shows month-over-month weakness, it signals demand-side slowdown that reduces earnings growth expectations, producing negative equity returns over 60 trading days as growth forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1.5% annualized and RSAFS month-over-month change < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2011 GDPC1 at 11206.1 billion, May 2011 RSAFS at 353866 million
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when both trigger conditions are met, or the interaction effect shows Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
